_about: planwright worker-settings profile (REQ-F1.8, D-38). A Claude Code settings fragment that a /orchestrate worker (background subagent or headless tmux/cron worker) runs under so the ROUTINE /execute-task toolset is pre-approved and the autonomy loop does not stall on permission prompts. planwright never edits settings.json itself (REQ-I1.2): merge this into the worker's settings (or reference it) the same way hook wiring is a manual step. SECURITY: this is a permissions allowlist and needs human sign-off before use; the deny block encodes planwright's hard invariants (never merge / force-push / amend / squash / rebase) as guardrails, but review the allow list against your repo's risk posture first. Deny precedence: Claude Code evaluates deny before allow, so the deny rules below override the broad `git push origin` allow regardless of specificity. The push denies are written to close every evasion of the force/main guardrails, not just the flag-immediately-after-`git push` form: `git push * --force*` / `git push * -f*` catch force flags placed AFTER the remote (e.g. `git push origin --force`); `git push *+*` catches the `+<refspec>` force form (e.g. `git push origin +HEAD:main`); `git push *:main` and `git push * main` catch any push whose destination is `main`; `--mirror`/`--all` are denied as bulk-ref escape hatches. Compound commands are parsed per-subcommand by Claude Code, so a denied push cannot be smuggled via `&&`/`;`/`|`/subshell.
